author: taulepton_
content: What is the relationship between guest and proof system versioning? Do we need to capture this in some sort of semver style design?

author: jsign
content:
I think hte relationship between the guest program and the underlying zkvm in the long term might be how they support the underlying zkevm standards, since that seems to me the "API boundary" they need to plug into to be compatible.

So say ethrex-33695e2 and zisk-v0.19 combination might be impossible, if they both don't implement the same zkevm standard version.

I think this is why we are trying to bundle both in this `ethrex-33695e2/zisk-v0.16.1` combined format that seems to fully describe both with a single string. I'm not entirely sure creating semver for these combinations might be useful, since the compatibility semantics of semver there i'm not sure would be useful for anything in particular (e.g. a verification key compatibility or generated proof compatibility -- but i could be missing something).

author: taulepton_
content: At a glance, I would have no way to know what API Ethrex implements using the commit digest. I was wondering if both guest / zkvm versioning follows some semver that reflect the API version boundary it supports? This would allow us to determine (at a glance) if the guest version is compatible with the ZKVM version. Thinking out loud here, don't have much experience in this domain.

author: jsign
content: Ah, I see what you mean. So maybe having a separate semver for guest program and zkvm releases (independent from their official semver), and maybe say major version of semver is tied to the zkevm standars, so that can help provers understand which guest and zkvms can be combined to create proofs? (i.e. not really a semver of the combinations themselves)

author: taulepton_
content: Yeah that’s the general direction I was thinking of

author: jsign
content: My first reaction is that might sound like a good idea, since makes compatibility between both things much more explicit. But prob can keep thinking if might have any drawback.
